name: cancel-mid-stream-cpu
description: Scenario B / I3 live cancellation workload against the REAL engine (inferbench -> infergate -> llama-server): every request streams and cancels after 8 client-observed output tokens (same trigger profile as Scenario A's cancel-mid-stream, new seed). 20 requests at 0.2 rps (~2.5 s busy time per request at ~1.6 s prefill + 8 tokens decode => ~0.25 utilization on 2 slots, so most cancels land while the engine decodes, some overlap another request's prefill). Engine-side release is observed via 25 ms /slots polling and llama-server 'srv stop: cancel task' log lines; requests the model ends by EOS before the 8th token complete ok (honest realized-cancel accounting). Expected release granularity is the engine's decode-batch boundary (infergate IG-T005 @74f2372: mid-stream 1.25-5.24 ms; up to seconds when the cancel lands inside another slot's prefill ubatch).
